query: US spot Bitcoin ETF net flows late June 2026 and early July 2026, weekly trend from Farside Investors and SoSoValue. Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29-July 3 2026? What is the Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line:

**Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29–July 3, 2026?** **Yes.** The week opened with a ~$231M outflow on June 29, extending a seventh consecutive week of net outflows. (Note: today is July 1, 2026, so only data through June 30 is final.)

**Weekly trend (Farside/SoSoValue/Galaxy):**
- Wk ending Jun 5: ~-$1.72B (peak)
- Jun 12: ~-$226M
- Jun 19: ~-$226M (sharp 87% slowdown)
- Jun 26: **re-accelerated to ~-$1.79B** (second-largest weekly redemption on record)
- Wk of Jun 29–Jul 3 (in progress): negative, opening -$231M

**Monthly:** June 2026 set a record **~-$4.06B** in net outflows — the worst month since the funds' Jan 2024 launch (beating Feb 2025's -$3.56B). IBIT was ~73% of it. Cumulative net inflows since launch remain positive at ~$53.4B.

**Bitcoin price trend (late June 2026):** Clear downtrend. BTC first closed below $60K on June 25 ($59,722) and ended the quarter at **$58,508 on June 30** (intraday low $58,202). June fell ~19%; H1 2026 closed with two consecutive losing quarters, ~50% below the Oct 2025 ATH of ~$126,080. Fear & Greed Index at 12 ("Extreme Fear"). Drivers: higher Treasury yields, fading Fed rate-cut odds (May PCE 4.1%), and US–Iran tensions.

**Caveat:** ETF flow data lags and revises — final figures should be confirmed against Farside/CoinGlass/SoSoValue trackers, and some IBIT outflows may reflect internal repositioning rather than wholesale exit.

key_facts: Net outflows ARE continuing into the week of June 29–July 3, 2026; the week opened with a ~$231M outflow on June 29, extending a seventh consecutive weekly-outflow streak.
June 2026 recorded a record ~$4.06B in net outflows (SoSoValue), the worst month since US spot Bitcoin ETFs launched in January 2024, beating the prior record of $3.56B (February 2025).
BlackRock's IBIT accounted for ~73% (~$3.3B) of June's outflows.
Weekly trend: ~-$1.72B (wk ending Jun 5) → ~-$226M (Jun 12) → ~-$226M (Jun 19) → re-accelerated to ~-$1.79B (Jun 26, second-largest weekly redemption on record).
Galaxy Research flagged a record -$6.35B 30-day net outflow, the worst of 582 rolling 30-day windows since launch.
Cumulative net inflows since Jan 2024 remain positive at ~$53.4B, down from a ~$63B peak in October 2025.
Bitcoin closed June 30, 2026 at $58,508, having first broken below $60,000 on June 25 ($59,722 close) for the first time since late 2024.
Bitcoin fell ~19% in June and closed H1 2026 with two consecutive losing quarters (Q1 ~-22%, Q2 ~-12 to -13%), only the third such occurrence in its history.
BTC traded ~49-53% below its October 6, 2025 all-time high of ~$126,080; Fear & Greed Index at 12 (Extreme Fear).
Largest single-day June outflow was ~$692-696M on June 25; June 26 saw -$499.6M (IBIT -$435.1M).
Outflow drivers: higher US Treasury yields, reduced Fed rate-cut expectations (May PCE 4.1% vs April 3.8%), and US-Iran geopolitical tensions.
ETF flow data lags and revises; some IBIT outflows may reflect internal repositioning rather than wholesale exit (per BlackRock's Jay Jacobs).
